examples: She wore a perpetually martyred expression.
Any water molecules that found their way to the floors of craters near the moon's poles, that water would be perpetually frozen, because the floors of those craters are always in shadow.
He was perpetually involving himself in this long lawsuit.
In these, as in the forests of the Old World, destruction was perpetually going on.
Perpetually compared with their established relatives, they're often held to higher standards than lesser-known peers.
